name: ragnarok
description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation extension for VSCode with local sentence transformers and semantic chunking

ragnarok.embeddingBackend.markdownDescription:
**Embedding backend** to use for generating vectors:

- **auto** (default): Try VS Code LM embeddings first; fall back to local HuggingFace if unavailable.
- **vscodeLM**: Force VS Code Language Model API embeddings (requires a registered embedding provider such as GitHub Copilot).
- **huggingface**: Force local HuggingFace Transformers.js embeddings (ONNX/WASM, fully offline).

⚠️ Switching backends may change the embedding dimension. Existing vector stores built with a different backend/model will need to be re-indexed.
enumDescriptions: Auto: Try VS Code LM first, fall back to HuggingFace
VS Code LM: Force VS Code Language Model API embeddings (requires provider)

ragnarok.embeddingVscodeModelId.markdownDescription:
**VS Code LM embedding model ID** to use when backend is `vscodeLM` or `auto`.

Leave empty to auto-select the first available model. The available models depend on the registered embeddings provider (e.g., GitHub Copilot).

💡 Check `Developer: Show Running Extensions` → Language Model API for available model IDs.

ragnarok.localModelPath.markdownDescription: Absolute path to a folder containing a locally downloaded Transformers.js-compatible embedding model. Models in this folder appear in the tree view and can be selected directly.

ragnarok.topK.description: Number of top results to return from RAG queries

ragnarok.chunkSize.description: Maximum size of text chunks for embedding (in characters)

ragnarok.logLevel.description: Logging verbosity level for the RAGnarōk output channel
enumDescriptions: Debug: Verbose logging for troubleshooting (most detailed)

ragnarok.retrievalStrategy.markdownDescription:
**Retrieval strategy** for RAG queries (applies to both simple and agentic modes):

- **hybrid** (recommended): 70% semantic + 30% keyword
- **vector**: Pure semantic similarity
- **ensemble**: Advanced RRF fusion of vector + BM25 (slower, more accurate)
- **bm25**: Pure keyword search with BM25 (no embeddings, fast exact matching)

💡 _Can be overridden per query via LM tool parameter_
enumDescriptions: Pure semantic search using embeddings only
Ensemble: Uses Reciprocal Rank Fusion with BM25 (most accurate, higher memory)
BM25: Pure keyword search (Okapi BM25 algorithm, no embeddings needed)

ragnarok.agenticMaxIterations.markdownDescription:
Maximum retrieval iterations in agentic mode. Higher = more thorough but slower.

⚠️ _Only applies when Agentic Mode is enabled_

ragnarok.agenticConfidenceThreshold.markdownDescription:
Confidence threshold (0-1) to stop retrieval. Lower = more iterations, higher = stops earlier.

⚠️ _Only applies when Agentic Mode is enabled_

ragnarok.agenticIterativeRefinement.markdownDescription:
Generate follow-up queries to fill information gaps detected during retrieval.

⚠️ _Only applies when Agentic Mode is enabled_

ragnarok.agenticLLMModel.markdownDescription:
**LLM model family** to use for query planning and evaluation.

Use **RAG: Select LLM Model** from the Command Palette to discover all available models at runtime.

💡 The available models depend on your Copilot subscription and installed extensions.

⚠️ _Only applies when LLM Mode is enabled_

ragnarok.agenticIncludeWorkspaceContext.markdownDescription:
**Include workspace context:** Pass selected code, active file, imports, and symbols to Copilot (like #codebase).

**Includes:**
- Selected code (max 1000 chars)
- File imports and symbols
- Workspace name

**Privacy:** Only relative paths, no sensitive data

⚠️ _Only applies when LLM Mode is enabled_

ragnarok.commonDatabasePath.markdownDescription:
**Path to a shared/common RAG database folder.** Topics from this path are read-only and cannot be deleted or modified. Useful for team-shared knowledge bases.

**Format:** Absolute path to a folder containing exported RAG database files.

viewsWelcome.contents:
RAGnarōk is starting up...

Ready in just a moment.

No topics yet.

Create a topic to start indexing your documents for semantic search and RAG queries with Copilot.

[$(add) Create Topic](command:ragnarok.createTopic)
[$(cloud-download) Import Topic](command:ragnarok.importTopic)

Need help? [Read the docs](https://github.com/hyorman/ragnarok#readme)

languageModelTools.name: ragQuery
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool when the user asks questions about topics they have added to the RAG system. Supports both simple single-shot retrieval and advanced agentic RAG with multi-step query planning, iterative refinement, and intelligent result evaluation. The tool intelligently matches the requested topic: it first tries an exact match, then finds the most semantically similar topic if no exact match exists. In agentic mode, complex queries are automatically decomposed into sub-queries for comprehensive information gathering. Always check the 'topicMatched' field and 'agenticMetadata' in the response.

topic.description: The name of the topic to search within (e.g., 'React Documentation', 'Company Policy'). If exact match is not found, the most semantically similar topic will be automatically selected.

query.description: The search query or question to find relevant information

topK.description: Number of top results to return (optional, uses configured value if not provided)

retrievalStrategy.description: Retrieval strategy: 'vector' (semantic only), 'hybrid' (semantic + keyword), 'ensemble' (RRF fusion), or 'bm25' (keyword only). Optional - uses configured value if not provided.

scripts.package: npm run package:win32-x64 && npm run package:win32-arm64 && npm run package:darwin-x64 && npm run package:darwin-arm64 && npm run package:linux-x64 && npm run package:linux-arm64
scripts.package:win32-x64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js win32-x64 && vsce package --target win32-x64
scripts.package:win32-arm64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js win32-arm64 && vsce package --target win32-arm64
scripts.package:darwin-x64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js darwin-x64 && vsce package --target darwin-x64
scripts.package:darwin-arm64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js darwin-arm64 && vsce package --target darwin-arm64
scripts.package:linux-x64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js linux-x64 && vsce package --target linux-x64
scripts.package:linux-arm64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js linux-arm64 && vsce package --target linux-arm64
scripts.publish: npm run publish:win32-x64 && npm run publish:win32-arm64 && npm run publish:darwin-x64 && npm run publish:darwin-arm64 && npm run publish:linux-x64 && npm run publish:linux-arm64
scripts.publish:win32-x64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js win32-x64 && vsce publish --target win32-x64
scripts.publish:win32-arm64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js win32-arm64 && vsce publish --target win32-arm64
scripts.publish:darwin-x64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js darwin-x64 && vsce publish --target darwin-x64
scripts.publish:darwin-arm64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js darwin-arm64 && vsce publish --target darwin-arm64
scripts.publish:linux-x64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js linux-x64 && vsce publish --target linux-x64
scripts.publish:linux-arm64: node scripts/install-platform-deps.js linux-arm64 && vsce publish --target linux-arm64
scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run compile:tests
